type.markdownDescription: Field type to use to index array of numbers for vector search. To learn more, see [vector Type](https://dochub.mongodb.org/core/avs-vector-type/).
type.description: Field type to use to index array of numbers for vector search.

path.markdownDescription: Name of the field to index. Use dot notation for embedded fields.
path.errorMessage: Field name does not meet MongoDB field naming restrictions. To learn more, see [Field Names](https://dochub.mongodb.org/core/mdb-field-names) .

numDimensions.markdownDescription: Number of vector dimensions. Value can be between 1 and 8192, both inclusive.
numDimensions.description: Number of vector dimensions. Value can be between 1 and 8192, both inclusive.

similarity.markdownDescription:
Vector similarity function to use to search for top K-nearest neighbors. 
Values include:
- `euclidean` - measures the distance between ends of vectors allowing similarity measurements based on varying dimensions. See [Euclidean](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclidean_distance) to learn more.
- `cosine` - measures similarity based on the angle between vectors allowing for similarity measurements not scaled by magnitude. See [Cosine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cosine_similarity) to learn more.
- `dotProduct` - measures similarity based on both the angle between, and magnitude of vectors. See [Dot Product](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dot_product) to learn more.
similarity.description: Vector similarity function to use to search for top K-nearest neighbors

quantization.description: Type of quantization to apply on the double or 32-bit float values in your vector field
quantization.markdownDescription:
Type of quantization to apply on the double or 32-bit float values in your vector field. 
Values include:
- `none` - for no quantization of the vector embeddings.
- `scalar` - for transforming 4 byte floating point values to 1 byte integers.
- `binary` - for transforming 4 byte floating point values to a single bit.

indexingMethod.markdownDescription:
Indexing method to use. 
Values include:
- `hnsw` - Hierarchical Navigable Small Worlds graph construction.
- `flat` - flat index build.
